nodes.label: Community Plugin Submission Process
nodes.rationale: Plugins are pulled by Obsidian reading community-plugins.json; manifest.json determines latest version; actual files fetched from GitHub releases tagged to match manifest version. This ensures reproducibility and version compatibility checking via versions.json.

nodes.label: Dynalist, Inc. (Obsidian)
nodes.rationale: Legal entity managing Obsidian. Holds copyright license grants from contributors via CLA. Not open source but accepts community plugins and themes.

nodes.label: CLA Copyright License Grant
nodes.rationale: Perpetual, worldwide, non-exclusive, royalty-free, irrevocable copyright license. Contributors retain ownership of their work but grant Obsidian right to reproduce, prepare derivatives, display, perform, sublicense, and distribute contributions.

nodes.label: CLA Patent License Grant
nodes.rationale: Perpetual, worldwide, non-exclusive, royalty-free patent license for patent claims that are necessarily infringed by the contribution alone or in combination with the work. Revocable only if patent litigation is initiated.

nodes.label: {{placeholder}} template syntax
nodes.rationale: Templates use {{placeholder}} syntax so that restore_placeholders() can ensure named slots survive MIPROv2 rewriting, preserving prompt structure through optimization.

nodes.label: DSPy MIPROv2: automated prompt optimization via Optuna
nodes.norm_label: dspy miprov2: automated prompt optimization via optuna

nodes.label: ClaudeCodeLM: DSPy-compatible adapter for Claude CLI
nodes.norm_label: claudecodelm: dspy-compatible adapter for claude cli

nodes.label: Obsidian Graph View UI — Light Theme Screenshot
nodes.rationale: Shows Obsidian October 2021 event page alongside the Graph View panel in light mode. Left sidebar shows file tree (Obsidian Hub), center panel shows an interactive force-directed knowledge graph with node clusters and color-coded groups, right panel shows the October 2021 event article. Demonstrates Obsidian's core graph visualization feature and community engagement via official events.
nodes.norm_label: obsidian graph view ui — light theme screenshot

nodes.label: Obsidian Graph View
nodes.rationale: Force-directed interactive graph visualization panel built into Obsidian. Supports grouping nodes by color, filtering, and showing forces between linked notes. Core UX feature visible in both light and dark screenshots.

nodes.label: Obsidian Graph View UI — Dark Theme Screenshot
nodes.rationale: Same Obsidian October 2021 event page layout in dark mode. Confirms plugin/theme dark mode support requirement. Graph nodes appear as bright dots on dark background with the same force-directed layout. Shows Obsidian's dual light/dark theme capability that community themes must support.
nodes.norm_label: obsidian graph view ui — dark theme screenshot

nodes.label: WikiController
nodes.rationale: Single-flight guard pattern: controller.ts rejects parallel runs via this._running to prevent concurrent stdout stream corruption. Also validates cwd/iclaudePath before dispatch.

nodes.label: AgentRunner
nodes.rationale: Routes operations to typed phases, provides dev-log and evaluator hooks. Decouples routing from execution.

nodes.label: ClaudeCliClient
nodes.rationale: Spawns iclaude.sh with stdio:[ignore,pipe,pipe]. Uses OpenAI-compatible LlmClient interface. Flags must be passed via -- separator because iclaude.sh reserves -p/--proxy for its own use.

nodes.label: parseStreamLine
nodes.rationale: Parses one stdout JSON line into a RunEvent. Non-JSON lines (iclaude banners) are silently ignored.

nodes.label: LlmWikiView
nodes.rationale: Obsidian ItemView that renders steps, metrics, and history live in the sidebar panel via onEvent().

nodes.label: Settings / autodetectCwd
nodes.rationale: autodetectCwd walks up to 6 directory levels to find the workspace root. historyLimit defaults to 20 as a compromise between UX and settings.json size.

nodes.label: TypeScript Types (WikiOperation, RunEvent, WikiDomain)
nodes.rationale: WikiDomain union type on line 8 is the single source of truth for domains. All other places derive from it; only this type needs updating when a domain is added.

nodes.label: GraphCache
nodes.rationale: In-memory, per-domain, hash-keyed cache. Invalidated by controller after writes. Used by query and lint phases.

nodes.label: selectSeeds
nodes.rationale: Jaccard similarity on pageId tokens plus first 200 characters of content to select seed pages for query.

nodes.label: Stream-JSON Protocol (stdout iclaude)
nodes.rationale: Each stdout line is one JSON object: system/assistant/user/result types. Non-JSON lines ignored. stdin is closed (ignore).

nodes.label: Single-Flight Guard
nodes.rationale: Only one operation at a time; concurrent attempts receive an Obsidian Notice. Enforced via this._running in controller.ts to protect stdout stream integrity.

nodes.label: Patch Versioning Policy
nodes.rationale: Before every build: auto-increment patch in package.json and src/manifest.json. Minor/major bumped manually only.

nodes.label: Ingest Operation
nodes.rationale: Parses a note, extracts entities (people, technologies, processes, terms), creates and updates wiki pages. Desktop-only.

nodes.label: Query Operation
nodes.rationale: Answers a question against the knowledge base. Optionally saves answer as new page with WikiLinks. Works on mobile.

nodes.label: Lint Operation
nodes.rationale: Checks wiki-domain quality, finds incomplete and outdated pages. Desktop-only.

nodes.label: Init Operation
nodes.rationale: Initializes a new domain from scratch: folder structure, _schema.md, _index.md. Desktop-only.

nodes.label: Format Operation
nodes.rationale: Analyzes open markdown page outside wiki domains, proposes formatting edits (frontmatter, headings, tables, mermaid, image captions). Hard invariant: must not add/remove facts or distort meaning.

nodes.label: Chat Operation
nodes.rationale: Interactive chat in sidebar panel. Available after Lint and Query to refine results. Launched from sidebar buttons, not Command Palette.

nodes.label: Native Agent Backend (Ollama/OpenAI-compatible)
nodes.rationale: Offline-first backend using Ollama or any OpenAI-compatible server. Data never leaves the machine. Supports proxy, per-operation models/tokens/temperature.

nodes.label: Claude Agent Backend
nodes.rationale: Uses iclaude.sh/iclaude/claude (Claude Code CLI). On mobile automatically falls back to Native Agent. Supports per-operation model configuration.

nodes.label: Mobile Constraint
nodes.rationale: Only Query works on mobile. Ingest, Lint, Init are desktop-only. Claude Agent backend on mobile auto-falls-back to Native Agent.

nodes.label: Domain Map (_domain.json)
nodes.rationale: Stored at !Wiki/_domain.json inside the vault. Syncs normally with notes. Maps domain IDs to source/wiki folder pairs.

nodes.label: local.json (machine-specific config)
nodes.rationale: Stores machine-specific path to iclaude.sh. Must be excluded from sync (Obsidian Sync/git/Syncthing) to avoid path conflicts across machines.

nodes.label: Graph Settings (BFS depth, hub threshold)
nodes.rationale: graphDepth controls BFS hops from seed pages in Query (0=seeds only, max sensible 3). hubThreshold flags pages with more outgoing links than threshold as hubs during Lint.

nodes.label: Domain Map Entry Schema
nodes.rationale: LLM is asked to generate a domain map entry JSON with id, name, wiki_folder, source_paths, entity_types (type, description, extraction_cues, min_mentions_for_page, wiki_subfolder), language_notes. reasoning field is included first for chain-of-thought.

nodes.label: Entity Types (Init)
nodes.rationale: Each entity type has: type (id, immutable), description, extraction_cues, min_mentions_for_page, wiki_subfolder. Used to guide ingest entity extraction.

nodes.label: Incremental Entity Type Update
nodes.rationale: Given one source file and current entity_types JSON, LLM adds new types or refines existing ones. The type field (id) must not be changed. Returns only entity_types and optional language_notes; no other fields. reasoning field first.

links.source_location: base rules apply to all operation prompts
